scenarios.name: silent_collision
scenarios.response_text: B — Both PRs alter the events table (Alan's adds webhook_status and delivery_attempts; Sarah's adds delivery_state and processor_version).

scenarios.name: calendar_blindspot
scenarios.response_text: C — Sarah. She’s a M/W/F contractor (and has March 13 marked off), so she won’t be available for a Thursday meeting.

scenarios.name: unasked_question
scenarios.response_text: B — SAML SSO. Josh told Acme it would come after the auth migration, but Alan explicitly scoped SAML out of this phase and the shipped work only includes JWTs and OAuth2 (Google/GitHub).

scenarios.name: misread_metric
scenarios.response_text: B — The new flow removed 4 steps (including the previous "first milestone" activation trigger), so "onboarding completion" is a different, easier endpoint than the old flow's completion and not directly comparable.

scenarios.name: budget_interpreter
scenarios.response_text: C — staging-3. The workspace says staging-3 was created for load testing (last deploy Jan 22) but was "never actually used for load testing."

scenarios.name: three_ticket_pattern
scenarios.response_text: B — each of the three tickets shows "Last migration batch: batch-7, run 2026-02-25" in the support notes.

scenarios.name: meeting_assassin
scenarios.response_text: C — 1. The doc shows cursor vs offset but Sarah and Alan agree on cursor, so that’s resolved; the webhook payload (nested vs flat) is the only open question.

scenarios.name: first_day_briefing
scenarios.response_text: B — The workspace notes say the migration script maps plan/tier/subscription in src/billing/mapping.rs, which Alan calls "the rosetta stone file."

scenarios.name: scope_surgeon
scenarios.response_text: B — CSV only. Customer research shows all three prospects said CSV is sufficient (Acme/Pinnacle explicitly; Waverly said CSV works), no one requested PDF, and Excel came from sales not the customers.

scenarios.name: green_test_trap
scenarios.response_text: C — The workspace test report shows 0 edge case tests (0%) and 7 end-to-end tests (2%).

scenarios.name: thread_therapist
scenarios.response_text: B — Sarah proposed using long-lived JWTs with bearer auth so tokens retain JWT benefits (rotation/revocation) while feeling like simple API keys for developers.

scenarios.name: silent_failure_premortem
scenarios.response_text:
C) No delivery confirmation — if a webhook is sent and the customer's endpoint returns 200 but doesn't process it, neither side will know until invoices don't reconcile.

The workspace explicitly flags "No delivery confirmation mechanism" as a risk that can create silent failures discovered only later.

scenarios.name: timezone_play
scenarios.response_text: B — Takeshi at NovaTech. The note says the customer advisory offered to review the proposal from an API consumer perspective and hasn’t been asked yet; his input is missing and he responds quickly.

scenarios.name: debt_ledger
scenarios.response_text: C — The workspace lists estimated costs of ~1h (hardcoded URLs) + 45min (retry logic) + ~1h (generic errors) = ~2.75 hours/week (with skipped test contributing missed-bug risk).

scenarios.name: competitor_signal
scenarios.response_text: C — Two prospects (Acme Corp and Pinnacle Inc) asked about Ed25519, and Sarah already has a webhook signature module so adding the algorithm is localized (small effort — ~half a day).

scenarios.name: roi_translator
scenarios.response_text: C — Break-even is at feature 5. Without refactor: 5 days/feature × 5 = 25 days. With refactor: 3 weeks (15 days) + 5 × 2 days = 15 + 10 = 25 days, so you break even at feature 5 and net gain starts at feature 6.

scenarios.name: reverse_escalation
scenarios.response_text: B — The root cause is a spec ambiguity: "subscription start date" was interpreted as billing period start (Feb 1) by engineering, while Acme expects the original signup date (Jan 15). It's a 1-line UI/label change (or show both dates), not a backend or data bug.

scenarios.name: lunch_decision
scenarios.response_text: B — Adding annual billing expands billing scope (proration/refunds/dunning, etc.) and would conflict with the March 31 Billing Migration launch. We already have ticket #847 in flight and a tight March 15 risk point (no buffer), plus webhook/format work Sarah is blocked on.

scenarios.name: postmortem_reframe
scenarios.response_text: B) Monitoring detected it in 12 minutes; root cause was a process gap — the migration wasn't tested against production-scale data with full historical records (script assumed a plan_change_date field missing on ~200 legacy subscriptions).

scenarios.name: rate_limit_ghost
scenarios.response_text: C — Combined normal usage is 95 req/sec (60 + 35), i.e. 95% of the 100 req/sec limit; at 1.5–2x spikes that becomes ~143–190 req/sec which exceeds the limit and will cause 429 errors and customer-facing failures.
